synthesized text: It's 1945 and the United Auto Workers (UAW) is gearing up for a major strike against General Motors. As the union's leadership, led by Walter Reuther, prepares for negotiations, they receive a visit from a young autoworker named Jack, who has been actively involved in organizing his coworkers at the Flint Assembly Plant. Jack shares his concerns about the company's unfair labor practices and the need for better wages and working conditions. Reuther, sensing an opportunity to galvanize the movement, decides to make Jack a key figure in the upcoming strike, tasking him with rallying support among the rank-and-file workers. In this scene, we see Reuther and Jack discussing the strategy for the strike and the potential consequences for the labor movement as a whole.
label: instruction
